name: Brined Dills
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and drain the cucumbers.
2. Place a layer of dill in a large crock.
3. Layer the cucumbers, dill, pickling spices (and whole rocambole and washed grape leaves if desired) in the crock.
4. Mix together the remaining ingredients and pour over the cucumbers until well covered. Make more brine as necessary.
5. Place a heavy plate on top of the cucumbers and weight it down with a large jar filled with water. Do not fill the crock to the top as it may overflow when the cucumbers release water.
6. Let sit for approximately 3 weeks, or until the cucumbers are uniformly colored throughout and well flavored with dill.
7. Remove the cucumbers from the brine and wash, discarding any soft cucumbers.
8. Pack pickles into clean quart jars adding 1-2 cloves of sliced rocambole (or garlic), 1 teaspoon peppercorns, 1/4 teaspoon pickling spices, and 1/2 teaspoon turmeric.
9. Prepare fresh brine from the recipe above, bring to a boil and pour over dills in jar leaving 1 inch headroom.
10. Seal jars and process in boiling water bath for 5 minutes. Start counting time when the jars are placed in boiling water.
11. Wait at least three weeks before eating.
